cards.title: Social Media Ban Improves Family Happiness
cards.body: A psychotherapist shares how limiting phone usage transformed her mood and relationships with her children. Strict boundaries on social media proved more effective than she expected.

cards.title: Parental Brains Stay Younger and Healthier
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional demands from parenting may actually strengthen cognitive function.

cards.title: Managing Parental Anxiety During Family Holidays
cards.body: A parent learned to manage constant worry while traveling with children, discovering she could handle real emergencies when they arose. Anxiety about vacations can prevent families from genuinely relaxing.

cards.title: Clear Limits Build Child Security Better Than Permissiveness
cards.body: Being overly permissive isn't always kind to children; clear, negotiated boundaries actually create security. Limits improve understanding and strengthen relationships between parents and kids.

cards.title: Early Developmental Histories Shape Child Assessment
cards.body: Understanding a child's early context is crucial for clinicians and families to properly assess development. A child's developmental story begins long before formal evaluation.

cards.title: Cursive Handwriting Skills Fading in Families
cards.body: Many parents and children are losing fluency in cursive writing, raising questions about lost valuable skills. The generational gap in handwriting ability reflects broader educational shifts.
